title: Tour de France 2026: Visma | Lease a Bike wins the first stage, Jonas Vingegaard in the yellow jersey, Remco Evenepoel 18 seconds back

news.title: Tour de France 2026: Visma | Lease a Bike wins the first stage, Jonas Vingegaard in the yellow jersey, Remco Evenepoel 18 seconds back

world.title: Tour de France 2026: Visma | Lease a Bike wins the first stage, Jonas Vingegaard in the yellow jersey, Remco Evenepoel 18 seconds back

environment.compositionIntent: A high-key matte paper surround carries the accepted Bridget Riley stripe field, warm yellow aperture, and pale negative space while leaving the dominant fracture-pressure band and near artist marks readable.

distanceSafety.reason: Canonical public camera retained as the fixed design bookmark: the accepted parts are broad frontal abstract fields with a central pressure subject, and no supplied evidence requires a portrait or close crop.

contrast.notes: Warm paper light protects the pale field while a restrained teal rim separates dark stripe and fracture marks without creating a generic glow layer.

paramSemantics.band: visual placement/readability band for full-world params; distinct from declared compositionOwnership.lane

owns: background atmosphere, palette/value field, shallow depth, and active negative space
surrounding WebGPU-safe environment behavior that gives other parts room to read
avoid: main news-pressure band unless explicitly assigned
main knot or focal tangle unless explicitly assigned

compositionAdaptations: Kept at the origin as the accepted background shell and surrounding WebGPU-safe environment field.
Opacity and motion are restrained so the environment supplies pale Riley stripe pressure, diagonal drift, and a warm central aperture without taking ownership of the main news-pressure band.
Kept as the background shell at origin with restrained opacity and low kinetic intensity so it supplies pale Riley stripe pressure and warm aperture support without owning the primary fracture band.
composition modified world-environment from isolated accepted-part defaults
workshopAcceptance: Inspected the provided local concept reference image directly; used it only as advisory palette/value/density guidance: high-key paper, dark vertical stripes, warm aperture, curved line pressure, and restrained teal accents.
Static check succeeded for drafts/world-environment.module.mjs with sourceHash f50140aecd44036f3c278a12d400421d5a7d2e763c2fc2613e8c44f0c194de41.
Preview attempt 2 succeeded with desktop screenshot previews/attempt-002/002-world-environment-draft-desktop.jpg and render report previews/attempt-002/desktop-render-report.json.
Render report status captured with readiness ready, rendererMode webgpu, rendererBackend webgpu, healthStatus ok, captureStatus ok.

Preview image showed a nonblank full-frame Bridget Riley-derived surround: high-key paper field, strong optical vertical stripe pressure, central warm yellow aperture/ring support, diagonal stage drift, and side stripe veils while leaving the main news band/knot to later parts.

paramSemantics.band: visual placement/readability band for full-world params; distinct from declared compositionOwnership.lane

owns: middleground selected-news pressure structure: dominant band, grid compression, barricade, or equivalent pressure form
avoid: full foreground artist mark field of loops, scratches, drips, splatters, and near-line behavior

compositionAdaptations: Held in the declared middleground ownership lane as the selected-news pressure structure.

Kept near center and set slightly deeper than attempt 1 so the accepted yellow target, figure-shaped aperture, vertical stripe compression, diagonal white bars, teal counterline, and concentrated fracture seam remain readable on mobile.
Motion intensity stays low and kinetic-diagonal, matching the Tour pressure cue without making the central Riley system unreadable.

Held as the central middleground news signal, slightly deeper and smaller than the first draft so the yellow target, figure aperture, diagonal fracture, and stripe compression stay readable on desktop and mobile.
composition modified non-literal-fracture-pressure from isolated accepted-part defaults
workshopAcceptance: Attempt 2 Playwright desktop preview captured successfully with healthStatus ok and WebGPU backend proof.
Screenshot shows a readable central yellow target with a figure-shaped aperture, full-width kinetic diagonals, vertical Riley stripe compression, and a concentrated fracture seam.
Render report records WebGPU-safe MeshBasicMaterial and LineBasicMaterial surfaces plus color-field/procedural-marks material-factory evidence.

owns: middleground selected-news pressure structure: dominant band, grid compression, barricade, or equivalent pressure form
avoid: full foreground artist mark field of loops, scratches, drips, splatters, and near-line behavior

paramSemantics.band: visual placement/readability band for full-world params; distinct from declared compositionOwnership.lane

owns: foreground artist mark and line behavior: loops, scratches, drips, splatters, dot/grid catches, and near readable marks
avoid: another dominant middleground news-pressure band, barricade/grid, or knot structure

compositionAdaptations: Kept in the declared foreground lane for readable artist mark behavior: short hard-edge scratches, contour catches, pale ticks, and teal/dark line disruptions.
Offset slightly right/lower and reduced after attempt 1 so the marks pressurize the fracture seam and lower target edge without burying the figure-shaped aperture.
Opacity and scale are held below the primary pressure signal to preserve the central aperture and yellow target on desktop and mobile.
Kept in the foreground mark role, reduced and offset right/lower so scratches pressurize the fracture seam without burying the figure-shaped aperture.
composition modified artist-mark-field from isolated accepted-part defaults
workshopAcceptance: Inspected the local concept reference image directly; used it only for broad pale paper, warm-earth, dark-line, stripe-density, and teal counterpoint cues.
Static validation succeeded for drafts/artist-mark-field.module.mjs with no errors.
Attempt 1 preview captured successfully but showed the part too small/dark in isolation, so the draft was revised.

Attempt 2 Playwright preview captured successfully with healthStatus ok and visible foreground Riley-style paper catches, contour loops, hard-edge diagonal marks, yellow leader marks, and right-side setback ticks.

Attempt 2 render report proved actual WebGPU backend and WebGPU-safe surfaces: MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, PointsMaterial, procedural-marks/soft-particles material-factory evidence, and utilities.createAnimatedInstancedMarkField.

owns: foreground artist mark and line behavior: loops, scratches, drips, splatters, dot/grid catches, and near readable marks
avoid: another dominant middleground news-pressure band, barricade/grid, or knot structure

compositionEvidence.draftIntent: Preserve the three accepted WebGPU-compatible Bridget Riley/Tour modules and assemble them as a central high-key pressure system: environment as pale stripe surround, non-literal-fracture-pressure as the middleground news signal, and artist-mark-field as the foreground disruption layer.
rendererCompatibilityPreserved: world-environment keeps accepted MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, PointsMaterial, sky-background-field, color-field, procedural-marks, soft-particles, and direction-space-skybox-shell evidence refs.
non-literal-fracture-pressure keeps accepted MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, color-field, and procedural-marks evidence refs.
artist-mark-field keeps accepted MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, PointsMaterial, and animated-instanced-mark-field evidence refs.

takeaways: Use the canonical fixed camera as the design bookmark and judge both desktop and mobile from that stable authored frame before changing projection.
Keep color ownership in world.outputColorTransform with one subtle final contrast/saturation/exposure/vignette grade, not stacked material or post claims.

Accept the final assembly only after Playwright desktop and mobile screenshots plus render reports show nonblank WebGPU captures with the central target, diagonal fracture, and foreground mark field readable.

image.altText: Tour de France 2026: Visma | Lease a Bike wins the first stage, Jonas Vingegaard in the yellow jersey, Remco Evenepoel 18 seconds back by Bridget Riley.
